title: Street Work & Reintegration
tagline: Reaching street children across Kisumu — building trust through patient outreach and guiding them back to safe, stable lives.
intro_p1: Street children are among the most vulnerable and invisible members of any urban community. In Kisumu, children end up on the streets for many reasons — family breakdown, poverty, abuse, or the loss of parents. Without intervention, many become trapped in cycles of survival that put them at serious risk.
intro_p2: UCDP's Street Work and Reintegration programme is one of our most long-standing and impactful initiatives. Our team of trained social workers and outreach volunteers go to where street children are — building genuine trust over time before offering support, services, and a path forward.
intro_p3: The ultimate goal is reintegration: connecting children with their families where safe, or with our Safe House and other support systems where family reunification is not possible.

impact_cards.label: We Go to Where the Children Are
For Those Without a Safe Family

steps.title: Street Outreach
steps.text: Our team visits locations where street children gather, making contact and building rapport over time before offering services.

steps.title: Trust Building
steps.text: We spend time building genuine trust with each child — understanding their story and circumstances before any intervention.

steps.title: Counselling & Support
steps.text: Children receive trauma-informed counselling, access to food, healthcare referrals, and emotional support.

steps.title: Reintegration
steps.text: Where safe, we work towards family reunification. For children without a safe family, we facilitate placement in our Safe House or appropriate settings.
